summary: Verified ADR 0156 live on current main by restoring the Headscale build-server route, confirming session-scoped remote workspaces and git metadata on docker-build-lv3, and clearing the lint debt that was blocking the first honest live apply.

verification.observed: On 2026-03-26 the controller rejoined the Headscale mesh as `100.64.0.3`, `https://headscale.example.com/health` returned HTTP 200, and `LV3_SESSION_ID=adr-0156-live make check-build-server` succeeded against `ops@10.10.10.30` through the `ops@100.64.0.1` jump path.

A direct remote probe returned `true`, `/home/ops/builds/platform_server/.lv3-session-workspaces/adr-0156-live/repo/.git-remote/worktree`, and `/home/ops/builds/platform_server/.lv3-session-workspaces/adr-0156-live/repo` for `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree && git rev-parse --git-dir && pwd`.

`LV3_SESSION_ID=adr-0156-live make remote-lint` completed successfully after the remote session workspace synced cleanly; the published ansible runner image still raised `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'ansible.parsing.yaml.constructor'`, so the command exercised its built-in local fallback and the final ansible-lint run finished with `Passed: 0 failure(s), 0 warning(s) on 681 files.`

notes: The live-apply branch aligned stale jump-host references to `ops@100.64.0.1`, preserved remote `.git-remote` metadata caches between runs, and removed the YAML and ansible-lint failures on current main that were blocking the ADR 0156 gate.
The production ansible check-runner image on `docker-build-lv3` still needs a refresh so `ansible-lint` can run remotely without relying on the command's documented local fallback path.
